executive_summary: Your Google Ads campaigns went live on April 21, 2026. We're now three days into the first month of live spend. Total invested so far: $158.74 across 433 ad impressions and 26 clicks. Click-through rate is 6.0% — roughly 50% above the contractor industry average, a strong early signal of ad relevance. No leads have converted yet, but at this click volume that's expected; a reliable conversion read needs 50+ clicks of data. The first 48 hours also surfaced two issues with how the account's keywords were configured — both have been corrected as of today, with a measurable cleanup effect expected in the next 48–72 hours.

findings.label: Finding 1 — Ad visibility constrained by bids, not budget
findings.body: Our Urgent Repairs campaign was eligible to enter roughly 1,700 auctions over the period, but only won ad placement in 22% of them. The remaining 78% split into two buckets: 3% lost due to hitting daily budget, and 75% lost because competitors were willing to pay more per click than our current $12 maximum bid. In short: budget is not the constraint — bids are.
findings.callout: Recommended action: raise the maximum cost-per-click cap from $12 to $16 on Urgent Repairs. Expected result: auction win rate climbs from 22% toward 30–40% without requiring additional budget.

findings.label: Finding 2 — Keyword list had inflated 7× beyond the plan
findings.body: Over the first 48 hours, Google's automated keyword suggestions inflated our Dry Rot ad group from the planned 4 keywords to 28 active keywords, most of them configured as "Broad Match" — which casts an extremely wide net. This caused ads to appear for irrelevant searches like "how to fix dry rot" (DIY instructions), "Trex deck colors" (product shoppers), and even competitor brand searches like "cf contracting" and "jeff myers construction."
findings.callout: Quantified waste: roughly 25% of the three-day spend (~$35–45) went to searches that will not convert to leads.

findings.label: Finding 3 — Broken keyword syntax in Composite Decks
findings.body: One keyword in the Composite Decks ad group was entered with incorrect syntax (missing a closing bracket), causing it to behave as Broad Match and capture dozens of "Trex brand research" searches — visitors shopping for deck materials, not contractor services. This single keyword pulled 30+ worthless impressions and was the primary reason ad delivery for Composite Decks collapsed after Day 1.

actions_completed: Dry Rot ad group — full reset. Deleted all 28 polluted keywords and re-added the 4 original plan keywords, all now on Phrase or Exact match (no Broad).

Composite Decks — broken keyword fixed. Removed the malformed Broad-match entry and replaced with the correct Exact-match version. Also restored 2 keywords that had gone missing from the original plan.
Stucco Repair — missing keyword added. Backfilled stucco contractors near me (Exact match) to bring the ad group up to the full 7-keyword plan.
Live performance telemetry. Connected a direct pipeline to the Google Ads API for day-over-day monitoring — no more manual screenshot exports.
actions_pending: Remove one remaining DIY keyword. A stray how to fix rotting door frame keyword survived the Dry Rot reset — needs to be deleted. Takes 30 seconds.

Disable Google's auto-apply recommendations. Critical. Without this step, Google will automatically re-suggest and re-add broad-match keywords within days, re-polluting the ad groups. This is the guardrail that prevents the bloat from returning.
Raise Urgent Repairs maximum CPC from $12 to $16. Directly addresses the 75% rank-lost-impression-share finding above.
expectations: Higher ad visibility. Impression share on Urgent Repairs should climb from 22% toward 30–40% within 48 hours of the bid raise.
Cleaner traffic quality. Click-through rate and Quality Score should both rise as junk queries (DIY, product research, competitor brand searches) are filtered out.
Potentially lower cost-per-click. Improved Quality Score can unlock better ad positions at the same or lower bid — a second-order benefit of the cleanup.

First lead conversions. At current click rates and expected 4–5% conversion rate, the first form submission or qualifying phone call should arrive within the next 50–75 clicks (roughly 2–4 more days).
next_review: The next check-in is planned for April 26 or 27, 2026 — 3 to 4 days from today. By then we'll have 72+ hours of clean post-cleanup data to measure the real effect of today's changes, enough click volume (projected 60–80 more clicks) to assess whether conversion rate is tracking to the 4–5% target, and visibility into whether the bid raise has closed the impression-share gap.
footer_note: Prepared April 23, 2026 · Data source: Google Ads live API (Customer ID 849-587-5417) · All figures verified as of 8:10 PM PT.
